what_it_does: This file is a design-system manifest for the ConceptVDesignSystem namespace. It catalogs preview cards (brand previews and UI-kit pages), global CSS files, a large set of design tokens (colors, shadows, spacing, radii, typography, animation, layout, and zone palettes), scoped theme overrides, and brand-font metadata.
observations: Contains 40 preview cards grouped into Brand, UI Kit — Platform, UI Kit — Vi, and UI Kit — Virtual Hub.
Lists two global CSS files: colors_and_type.css and styles.css.
Defines ~140 design tokens, mostly CSS custom properties, with kind, name, value, and source file.
Includes a warm brand palette (paper, ink, gold, bronze) plus semantic status colors.
Uses CSS color-mix(in oklch, ...) for a zone-based color system driven by --zone-intensity and per-zone pig colors.
Provides scoped palette overrides via .zone-palette-cooler and .zone-palette-warmer that redefine the same --pig-* token names.
Includes three UI-kit high-level pages: Platform dashboard, Vi AI workspace, and Virtual Hub viewer.
Brand fonts Sora, DM Sans, and JetBrains Mono are declared ok and mapped to token usages.
components, startingPoints, and fonts arrays are empty.
Some token names are duplicated with different values and scopes (e.g., --pig-base appears three times).
Spacing tokens --s-8 and --s-7 both map to 32px; --s-10 and --s-9 both map to 40px.
standouts: Zone color system using oklch color-mix and intensity variables for programmatic surface/edge/ink generation.
Scoped palette overrides (.zone-palette-cooler / .zone-palette-warmer) redefining the same pig tokens.
Three distinct UI-kit contexts (Platform, Vi AI workspace, Virtual Hub) within one design-system manifest.
99 SVG icon library referenced as a single source of truth.
Vi AI framework mark and emoji-as-nav-icons as explicit brand decisions.
conventions: Follows a manifest schema with namespace, cards, tokens, themes, brandFonts, and globalCssPaths.
Tokens use semantic CSS custom-property naming (e.g., --paper, --ink, --accent-gold, --status-success).
Each token records kind, definedIn file, and optional scope/annotation.
Spacing scale is based on an 8-px grid with named steps.
Shadows share a single warm near-black tint for cohesion.
Cards include path, group, viewport, subtitle, and name for preview/discovery.
concerns: Duplicate token names with different values and scopes may be ambiguous for consumers that ignore scope.
Empty components and startingPoints arrays leave the manifest without concrete component metadata.
Spacing tokens --s-8/--s-7 and --s-10/--s-9 have identical values, which may be a mistake or intentional alias.
The meaning of source: "spa" is not explained in this file.
No version, timestamp, or maintainer fields are present.
notes: A token-heavy design-system manifest that pairs brand preview pages with a sophisticated zone-based color-mix theming system and three product UI-kit entry points.
questions: What does source: "spa" signify in this context?
Are the duplicate spacing values (32px, 40px) intentional aliases or errors?
How are the scoped .zone-palette-cooler and .zone-palette-warmer classes intended to be applied?
Why are components and startingPoints empty despite the design-system scope?
How are the preview cards generated or kept in sync with the actual preview HTML files?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (design-system manifest) matches the actual content: it catalogs tokens, themes, fonts, and preview pages. However, it lacks component definitions and starting points, so it is more of a token-and-preview catalog than a full component library manifest.
apparent_themes: Warm, editorial brand aesthetic (paper, ink, gold, bronze).
Semantic, token-driven design with systematic scales for spacing, radii, type, and elevation.
Zone-based color theming for distinct functional areas.
AI-assisted workspace as a first-class product surface (Vi).
Multi-product design system serving Platform, Vi, and Virtual Hub contexts.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to establish a single source of truth for a multi-product design system, making tokens, themes, and preview pages discoverable while supporting programmatic palette variation through intensity-scoped color mixing.
novelty: The zone palette system that derives surface/strong/edge/ink colors from base pig colors using oklch color-mix and an intensity variable, plus scoped class overrides that redefine the same token names for cooler or warmer palettes.
contribution: This file contributes a structured design-system ledger with a rich token taxonomy, scoped theme overrides, and a navigable preview-card index that a fused system could retain for discovery, theming, and cross-product consistency.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design-system manifest for ConceptVDesignSystem that lists preview cards, global CSS files, a large set of semantic design tokens including an oklch color-mix zone palette system, scoped palette overrides, and brand-font metadata.
intention_for_embedding: This manifest was intended to serve as a discoverable, token-driven index of a multi-product design system covering brand guidelines, component previews, and three UI-kit contexts.
